codeReview.summary: Reviewed the worker handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit 7f6964d759c3149a33a41936f5b9dd88c790e417 scoped diff for core/src/repository/mod.rs. The feature stays within scope: save_many now converts each RecordId into a stable serialized String before duplicate tracking, which removes clippy::mutable_key_type exposure while preserving the existing duplicate-record conflict path and transactional cleanup semantics.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill focuses on fail-first implementation, but this feature was an incidental validator fix already present in the working tree, so the worker reasonably used a verification-and-land path; that exception is only hinted at, not clearly documented as a standard procedure.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared library notes cover sensitive-crypto behavior but do not capture the repository-specific invariant exercised here: save_many duplicate detection keys should avoid mutable-key types and use a stable value representation instead of RecordId directly.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The accepted fix in C:\Users\admin\appdb\core\src\repository\mod.rs adds record_id_to_stable_key() and switches save_many duplicate tracking from HashSet<RecordId> to HashSet<String>; no note in C:\Users\admin\appdb\.factory\library\architecture.md or related library files documents this repository pattern.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, shared-state files, and the exact commit diff. The implementation is correctly scoped and preserves save_many semantics while fixing the clippy mutable-key issue; I found no blocking or non-blocking code issues.
